title: Question: Alternative $display flush strategy
body:
---


Author Name: **R. Diez**
Original Redmine Message: 931 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Flushing the $display output tends to be problematic:

- If you don't flush and the simulation hangs, you don't see the last lines. If you kill the hung simulation, you loose them.

- If you do flush with --autoflush, performance suffers.

The following alternative strategies could help:

1) Time-based flush, say once per second.

2) Iteration-based flush, say once every 100 iterations.

3) The user can flush at will:

a) In Verilog code, like Icarus Verilog with "$fflush(0); $fflush(1)", which I believe does not work with Verilator, or does it?

b) In the main() user-supplied function in the top-level C++ module.
Verilator needs to document that a "fflush (stdout);" does the trick. However, that could change in the future, so it would be best to provide a Verilator-specific wrapper for flushing purposes.
